docs.title: indexio
docs.text:
Lightweight semantic indexing and retrieval for project knowledge sources.

Indexes document sources (markdown, code, YAML, etc.) into a ChromaDB vector store and provides semantic search. Standalone library — no knowledge of projio or other ecosystem packages.

docs.title: Current status
docs.text: Version: 0.1.0 (scaffolded, not yet published to PyPI)

docs.title: What works
docs.text:
• Config system — YAML-based config (IndexioConfig) with explicit root argument, no auto-detection. Supports includes: for config composition and multiple named stores.
  • Build pipeline — build_index() reads source globs or paths, splits documents with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, batches embeddings via HuggingFace, upserts into Chroma. Full rebuild (clears store) and partial rebuild (by source id) both supported.
  • Query — query_index() and query_index_multi() with optional corpus filter, deduplication by source path + chunk index.
  • Edit helpers — sync_owned_sources() / replace_owned_sources() for programmatic YAML config management (used by other ecosystem tools that register their own sources).
  • CLI — indexio init / build / query / status / serve with --root, --store, --sources, --corpus, --k, --json flags.
  • Chat server — indexio.chat subpackage providing a unified RAG chatbot for all projio subsystems. FastAPI backend with /chat/ endpoint, pluggable LLM backends (Ollama, OpenAI-compatible), and a reusable JS/CSS frontend widget. Install with pip install indexio[chat], run with indexio serve.
  • Package structure — src/ layout, pyproject.toml, Makefile following biblio/notio conventions.

docs.title: What is not yet done
docs.text:
• Test coverage is still partial; chat and end-to-end integration coverage need expansion
  • Not published to PyPI
  • Embedding model is hardcoded to all-MiniLM-L6-v2; no pluggable embedding backend yet
  • No incremental/delta indexing (modified-file detection)
  • No support for binary or PDF sources

docs.title: Near-term (v0.1.x)
docs.text:
• [ ] Write a test suite (tests/) covering config parsing, build pipeline (with a fixture directory), and query round-trip
  • [ ] Write tests for the chat pipeline and endpoint
  • [ ] Publish to PyPI as indexio 0.1.0
  • [ ] Add indexio status --json output for machine consumption
  • [ ] Integrate the chatbot widget into biblio, codio, and notio docs sites

docs.title: Medium-term (v0.2)
docs.text:
• [ ] Incremental indexing — track file mtimes or content hashes in a sidecar JSON; only re-embed changed files on rebuild
  • [ ] PDF/docling source type — add a type: pdf source that runs docling extraction before chunking
  • [ ] Pluggable embeddings — allow embedding_backend: openai or embedding_backend: ollama in config, not just HuggingFace
  • [ ] Multi-store query — query_index_stores() that fans out across multiple stores and merges results

docs.title: Longer-term (v0.3+)
docs.text:
• [ ] Watch mode — indexio watch --config ... to re-index on file changes
  • [ ] Export — dump indexed chunks to JSONL for analysis or migration
  • [ ] Metadata filtering — richer filter expressions beyond corpus (e.g. by source_id, date range)
  • [ ] Remote stores — support Chroma HTTP client (not just local persist) as a store backend

docs.title: Explanation
docs.text: Understanding-oriented discussion that clarifies concepts.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: indexio is a lightweight semantic indexing and retrieval library organized into five modules.

docs.title: Pipeline
docs.text: YAML Config → Load Sources → Read Files → Split into Chunks → Embed → Upsert to ChromaDB

docs.title: Modules
docs.text:
config — Loads and validates YAML configuration. Supports composition via includes: for merging configs from multiple ecosystem packages. Resolves stores and sources into typed dataclasses.

build — Reads files matched by source globs or paths, splits text with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, generates embeddings via HuggingFace, and upserts into ChromaDB in batches of 100. Supports full rebuild (clear + reindex) and partial rebuild (by source id).

query — Wraps ChromaDB similarity search with optional corpus filtering and deduplication by (source_path, chunk_index). query_index_multi fans out multiple queries and merges results.

edit — Programmatic config manipulation. sync_owned_sources lets ecosystem packages register their sources without overwriting each other. The caller declares which source ids it owns; all others are preserved.

cli — Thin CLI layer exposing init, build, query, status, and serve commands.

docs.title: Key design decisions
docs.text:
• Config-first: all behavior is driven by YAML. No auto-detection of project structure.
  • Explicit root: the project root is always passed as an argument, never inferred.
  • Standalone: indexio has no knowledge of projio, codio, biblio, or notio. It just indexes files and answers queries.
  • Lazy imports: heavy dependencies (langchain, chromadb, huggingface) are imported inside functions to keep import time fast.

docs.title: Role In The Projio Ecosystem
docs.text: indexio provides the shared retrieval layer for the projio ecosystem.

docs.title: The pattern
docs.text:
Each ecosystem package owns its content and registers sources into a shared indexio config:

  • codio registers .codio/**/*.md and catalog.yml → corpus codelib
  • biblio registers bib/derivatives/docling/**/*.md → corpus biblio_docling
  • notio notes are indexed → corpus notes

indexio does not know what the content means. It chunks, embeds, and searches.

docs.title: Why a shared layer
docs.text:
If each package managed its own RAG:

  • Duplicate infrastructure (three copies of ChromaDB, embedding models, chunking logic)
  • No cross-corpus search (an agent asking "what do we know about X?" would need three separate calls)
  • Inconsistent embeddings across corpora make similarity scores incomparable
  • Resource waste from multiple embedding models loaded in memory

docs.title: The contract
docs.text:
codio, biblio, notio  →  content owners (know WHAT to index)
indexio               →  retrieval engine (knows HOW to index)
projio MCP server     →  query surface (knows WHO is asking)

Each package has a rag sync command that calls sync_owned_sources. This is the entire integration surface. indexio never imports ecosystem packages; they never import indexio at build time.

docs.title: Source registration flow
docs.text:
1. Package calls sync_owned_sources(config_path, root, owned_source_ids=[...], sources=[...])
  2. indexio reads the config, replaces only the caller's sources, preserves everything else
  3. On indexio build, all registered sources are indexed into a single ChromaDB store
  4. On indexio query, results from all corpora are returned (optionally filtered by corpus)

docs.title: How-To Guides
docs.text: Task-oriented guides that help you solve a specific problem.

docs.title: Full rebuild
docs.text:
Clears the store and re-indexes all sources:

indexio build

docs.title: Partial rebuild (specific sources)
docs.text:
Re-index only selected sources without clearing the rest:

indexio build --sources docs,notes

docs.title: Target a specific store
docs.text: indexio build --store local

docs.title: JSON output
docs.text:
indexio build --json

Returns a JSON summary with per-source stats (files, chars, chunks).

docs.title: Run The Chat Server
docs.text: The chat server provides a RAG-powered Q&A endpoint and an embeddable web widget.

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install indexio[chat]

docs.title: Start the server
docs.text:
indexio serve

Then open the URL printed by the server, such as http://localhost:9100/. If the requested port is already in use, indexio serve will automatically pick the next free port and print it.

docs.title: Custom LLM backend
docs.text:
# OpenAI-compatible API
indexio serve \
    --llm-backend openai --llm-model gpt-4 \
    --llm-base-url https://api.openai.com

# Local Ollama (default)
indexio serve \
    --llm-backend ollama --llm-model llama3

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
All settings can be overridden via INDEXIO_CHAT_* environment variables:

INDEXIO_CHAT_PORT=8080
INDEXIO_CHAT_LLM_MODEL=mistral

docs.title: Embed the widget
docs.text:
<script>
  window.INDEXIO_CHAT = {
    apiUrl: "http://localhost:9100/chat/",
    title: "My Docs Assistant",
  };
</script>
<script src="http://localhost:9100/chatbot/chatbot.js"></script>
<link href="http://localhost:9100/chatbot/chatbot.css" rel="stylesheet">

docs.title: Write the default config
docs.text:
indexio init --root /path/to/project

This creates .indexio/config.yaml with sensible defaults.

docs.title: Custom output path
docs.text: indexio init --output config/indexio.yaml

docs.title: Overwrite an existing config
docs.text: indexio init --force

docs.title: Basic query
docs.text: indexio query "semantic search"

docs.title: Filter by corpus
docs.text: indexio query --corpus docs "semantic search"

docs.title: Adjust number of results
docs.text: indexio query --k 12 "semantic search"

docs.title: JSON output
docs.text: indexio query --json "semantic search"

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from indexio import query_index, query_index_multi

# Single query
result = query_index(
    config_path=".indexio/config.yaml",
    root="/path/to/project",
    query="embeddings",
    corpus="docs",
    k=8,
)

# Multi-query with deduplication
result = query_index_multi(
    config_path=".indexio/config.yaml",
    root="/path/to/project",
    queries=["embeddings", "vector search"],
    k=5,
)

docs.title: Register Sources From Other Packages
docs.text: Ecosystem packages (codio, biblio, notio) register their own sources in a shared indexio config using sync_owned_sources.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from indexio import sync_owned_sources

result = sync_owned_sources(
    config_path=".indexio/config.yaml",
    root="/path/to/project",
    owned_source_ids=["my-notes", "my-catalog"],
    sources=[
        {"id": "my-notes", "corpus": "notes", "glob": ".myapp/**/*.md"},
        {"id": "my-catalog", "corpus": "catalog", "glob": ".myapp/catalog.yml"},
    ],
)

print(result.added)    # source ids that were new
print(result.updated)  # source ids that changed
print(result.removed)  # source ids no longer provided

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. The config file is read (or created from the default template if missing).
  2. Sources whose id is in owned_source_ids are replaced with the new sources list.
  3. Sources not owned by the caller are preserved untouched.
  4. The config is written back to disk.

This allows multiple packages to coexist in the same config without overwriting each other's sources.

docs.title: Initializing a config that doesn't exist yet
docs.text:
Pass force_init=True to always start from the default template:

sync_owned_sources(
    config_path=".indexio/config.yaml",
    root=".",
    owned_source_ids=["my-src"],
    sources=[{"id": "my-src", "corpus": "c", "glob": "*.md"}],
    force_init=True,
)

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
• Commit
  • Daily
  • Idea
  • Issue
  • Meeting
  • Personal
  • Weekly

docs.title: Contents
docs.text:
• idea-arash-20260312-175539-442171 title:pluggable-rag-engines
  • idea-arash-20260312-163249-548902 title:idea

docs.title: idea
docs.text:
Since all the subsystems of projio: biblio, codio, notio would benefit from a RAG system which is supposed to be provided by indexio infrastructure. all also need a chatbot

see /storage2/arash/sirocampus has a chatbot /storage2/arash/projects/cogpy also has a chatbot.

Let's try to unify the chatbot for all the subsystems of projio through indexio infrastructure.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
• Unified chatbot added to indexio as the indexio.chat subpackage
  • Any projio subsystem can reuse the same backend + frontend widget
  • RAG pipeline: retrieve from indexio vector store, generate via configurable LLM (Ollama or OpenAI-compatible)
  • Frontend: reusable JS/CSS widget with persistent localStorage state and markdown rendering

docs.title: New files
docs.text:
• src/indexio/chat/__init__.py — public API
  • src/indexio/chat/models.py — Pydantic ChatRequest, ChatResponse, SourceRef
  • src/indexio/chat/pipeline.py — RAG pipeline (retrieve + LLM generate)
  • src/indexio/chat/settings.py — ChatSettings (env-configurable via INDEXIO_CHAT_*)
  • src/indexio/chat/app.py — FastAPI app with /chat/ endpoint + /health
  • src/indexio/chat/static/chatbot.js — reusable frontend widget
  • src/indexio/chat/static/chatbot.css — widget styles

docs.title: CLI
docs.text: • indexio serve starts the chat server using the default project root and config path

docs.title: Install
docs.text: • pip install indexio[chat] pulls in fastapi, uvicorn, pydantic-settings

docs.title: Usage in subsystems
docs.text:
Each subsystem's docs site includes the widget:

<script>
  window.INDEXIO_CHAT = {
    apiUrl: "http://localhost:9100/chat/",
    title: "Biblio Assistant",
    storageKey: "biblio_chat_v1",
  };
</script>
<script src="http://localhost:9100/chatbot/chatbot.js"></script>
<link href="http://localhost:9100/chatbot/chatbot.css" rel="stylesheet">

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [x] Unify chatbot from sirocampus/cogpy into indexio
  • [x] RAG pipeline with pluggable LLM backends (Ollama, OpenAI-compat)
  • [x] Reusable frontend widget (JS + CSS)
  • [x] CLI serve command
  • [x] Optional [chat] dependency group
  • [ ] Add tests for the chat pipeline
  • [ ] Integrate into biblio, codio, notio docs

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• LLM backends: ollama (default, local) and openai (any OpenAI-compatible API)
  • Frontend widget is configurable via window.INDEXIO_CHAT global before script load
  • All settings overridable via INDEXIO_CHAT_* environment variables
  • The widget serves from the indexio chat server at /chatbot/ static mount

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
indexio currently hand-rolls its retrieval pipeline (langchain-text-splitters, langchain-chroma, langchain-huggingface). Instead of maintaining this plumbing, indexio could delegate to external high-level RAG engines — either a single default engine or corpus-specific engines chosen for their strengths.

The key constraint: indexio's value is the config layer and ecosystem contract (YAML source registration, multi-store, sync_owned_sources), not the retrieval internals. The engine should be swappable without changing the surface API.

docs.title: General-purpose (default engine candidate)
docs.text:
• txtai — github.com/neuml/txtai
  • Closest match to indexio's philosophy: YAML-configurable, lightweight, embeddings DB with built-in semantic search
  • Supports hybrid search (sparse + dense), graph networks, SQL queries
  • Handles chunking, embedding, and indexing in one package

  • Would replace all four langchain deps with one

  • FlashRAG — github.com/RUC-NLPIR/FlashRAG

  • Research toolkit (EMNLP 2025) with Chunkie chunking library
  • Worth studying for chunking strategies even if not used as engine

docs.title: Optimized for document/PDF output (biblio corpus)
docs.text:
• LightRAG — github.com/HKUDS/LightRAG
  • Graph-augmented retrieval: dual-level (chunk + inter-dependency)
  • Incremental indexing without full rebuild — relevant for growing paper corpora

  • Heavier LLM requirements (32B+ recommended for entity extraction)

  • RAG-Anything — github.com/HKUDS/RAG-Anything

  • Multimodal RAG from the same team as LightRAG
  • Could handle docling derivatives with tables/figures better than pure text search

docs.title: Optimized for code (codio corpus)
docs.text:
• Code-specific embedding models (e.g., jinaai/jina-embeddings-v3, Salesforce/SFR-Embedding-2) may outperform general-purpose models on code search
  • Worth evaluating whether codio corpus needs a different embedding strategy vs. just a different model within the same engine

docs.title: Storage-efficient / local-first
docs.text:
• LEANN — github.com/yichuan-w/LEANN
  • 97% storage savings via pruned graph + on-demand recomputation
  • Relevant if indexio targets large local corpora

docs.title: A. Single engine, swap internals (recommended starting point)
docs.text:
Replace langchain deps with txtai. Keep the same config surface. Simplest path.

indexio config (YAML) → txtai Embeddings → search API

docs.title: B. Engine-per-corpus (future option)
docs.text:
Each corpus can declare its engine in config. indexio routes queries to the right backend and merges results.

sources:
  - id: docs
    corpus: docs
    engine: txtai          # default

  - id: biblio_docling
    corpus: papers
    engine: lightrag       # graph-augmented for papers

  - id: codio-notes
    corpus: codelib
    engine: txtai
    embedding_model: jinaai/jina-embeddings-v3   # code-tuned model

This is more complex but lets each corpus use what works best.

docs.title: C. Federated query layer (most ambitious)
docs.text:
indexio becomes a query router. Each ecosystem package can bring its own engine. indexio only owns the config registry and query fan-out/merge.

Risk: leaky abstraction, hard to keep result scores comparable across engines.

docs.title: Decision Criteria
docs.text:
• How much does retrieval quality vary across engines for our corpus types?
  • Is the complexity of multi-engine worth it, or does a single good engine with per-corpus embedding models cover 90% of the need?
  • How stable are the candidate engines' APIs? (txtai has been stable for years; LightRAG is newer and research-driven)

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
• [ ] Benchmark txtai vs current langchain pipeline on existing corpora
  • [ ] Test code-specific embedding models on codio corpus
  • [ ] Evaluate LightRAG on biblio docling output
  • [ ] Prototype Strategy A (txtai swap) — measure LOC reduction and quality
  • [ ] If quality differs significantly by corpus, prototype Strategy B

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The ecosystem contract (sync_owned_sources, YAML config, rag_query API) must remain stable regardless of engine choice
  • codio, biblio, notio should never need to change when the engine changes
  • Start with Strategy A; only move to B if benchmarks show meaningful quality gaps across corpus types

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: Information-oriented technical descriptions.

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
All public symbols are exported from the indexio package:

from indexio import (
    IndexioConfig,
    StoreConfig,
    SourceConfig,
    load_indexio_config,
    build_index,
    sync_owned_sources,
    query_index,
    query_index_multi,
)

docs.title: load_indexio_config(config_path, root) → IndexioConfig
docs.text: Load and parse a YAML config file. Resolves includes composition, validates stores and sources.

docs.title: build_index(*, config_path, root, store=None, sources_filter=None) → dict
docs.text:
Build or rebuild the Chroma index. Returns a summary dict with per-source stats.

  • Full rebuild (default): clears the store before indexing.
  • Partial rebuild: pass sources_filter=["docs"] to only rebuild specific sources.

docs.title: query_index(*, config_path, root, query, store=None, prefer_canonical=False, corpus=None, k=4) → dict
docs.text: Run a similarity search. Returns a dict with results list, each containing corpus, source_id, source_path, chunk_index, and snippet.

docs.title: query_index_multi(*, config_path, root, queries, store=None, prefer_canonical=False, corpus=None, k=4) → dict
docs.text: Run multiple queries and merge results with deduplication by (source_path, chunk_index).

docs.title: sync_owned_sources(config_path, root, *, owned_source_ids, sources, force_init=False, template=None) → OwnedSourcesSyncResult
docs.text: Register sources in a shared config. Only sources whose id is in owned_source_ids are modified; all others are preserved.

docs.title: IndexioConfig
docs.text: Field Type root Path config_path Path embedding_model str chunk_size_chars int chunk_overlap_chars int default_store str canonical_store str \| None stores dict[str, StoreConfig] sources list[SourceConfig]

docs.title: StoreConfig
docs.text: Field Type name str persist_directory Path read_only bool description str \| None

docs.title: SourceConfig
docs.text: Field Type id str corpus str path str \| None glob str \| None exclude tuple[str, ...]

docs.title: indexio init
docs.text:
Write a starter config into a project.

Flag Default Description --root . Project root --output .indexio/config.yaml Output path relative to root --force Overwrite an existing file

docs.title: indexio build
docs.text:
Build a Chroma index from an indexio config.

Flag Default Description --config .indexio/config.yaml Path to the indexio config file --root . Project root --store Named store from the config --sources Comma-separated source ids for partial rebuild --json Print JSON summary

docs.title: indexio query
docs.text:
Query the Chroma index.

Flag Default Description --config .indexio/config.yaml Path to the indexio config file --root . Project root --store Named store from the config --corpus Corpus filter --k 8 Number of results --json Emit JSON output query (positional) Query text

docs.title: indexio status
docs.text:
Show index status for each configured store.

Reports source state without rebuilding by comparing current file matches against the last build manifest stored next to the local index.

Flag Default Description --config .indexio/config.yaml Path to the indexio config file --root . Project root

docs.title: indexio serve
docs.text:
Start the chat server (requires indexio[chat]).

Flag Default Description --config .indexio/config.yaml Path to the indexio config file --root . Project root --store Named store --corpus Corpus filter for retrieval --host 0.0.0.0 Bind host --port 9100 Preferred bind port; falls forward to the next free port if occupied --llm-backend ollama LLM backend: ollama or openai --llm-model llama3 LLM model name --llm-base-url http://localhost:11434 LLM API base URL --title Docs Assistant Chat widget title

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: indexio uses a YAML config file. The default location is .indexio/config.yaml.

docs.title: Full schema
docs.text:
# Optional: compose from other configs
includes:
  - path/to/base.yaml

# Embedding model (HuggingFace model name)
embedding_model: "sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2"

# Chunking parameters
chunk_size_chars: 1000
chunk_overlap_chars: 200

# Store selection
default_store: local
canonical_store: shared    # optional, preferred for reads if it exists

# Named stores
stores:
  local:
    persist_directory: .cache/indexio/chroma_db
    read_only: false
    description: "Per-clone writable Chroma cache"
  shared:
    persist_directory: /data/shared/chroma_db
    read_only: true
    description: "Read-only canonical store"

# Document sources
sources:
  - id: docs
    corpus: docs
    glob: "docs/**/*.md"
    exclude:
      - "docs/drafts/**"

  - id: readme
    corpus: docs
    path: README.md

docs.title: Top-level keys
docs.text: Key Type Default Description includes list[str] [] Paths to other YAML configs to merge (relative to this file) embedding_model str sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 HuggingFace model name chunk_size_chars int 1000 Max chunk size in characters chunk_overlap_chars int 200 Overlap between adjacent chunks default_store str first store Store used by default canonical_store str none Preferred store for reads (if it exists on disk)

docs.title: Store definition
docs.text: Key Type Required Description persist_directory str yes Path to ChromaDB directory (relative to project root) read_only bool no If true, build refuses to write to this store description str no Human-readable label

docs.title: Source definition
docs.text:
Each source must define exactly one of path or glob.

Key Type Required Description id str yes Unique identifier for this source corpus str yes Corpus tag stored in chunk metadata glob str one of Glob pattern relative to project root path str one of Single file path relative to project root exclude list[str] no Glob patterns to exclude (applied to relative paths)

docs.title: Config composition
docs.text:
Use includes to merge configs from multiple ecosystem packages:

includes:
  - bib/config/indexio.yaml
  - .codio/indexio.yaml

stores:
  local:
    persist_directory: .cache/indexio/chroma_db

Merge rules:

  • stores: merged by name (overlay wins on conflict)
  • sources: merged by id (overlay wins on conflict, new ids appended)
  • scalars: overlay replaces base
  • includes key itself is never propagated into the merged result

docs.title: Tutorials
docs.text: Learning-oriented walkthroughs that take you through a series of steps.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: This tutorial walks you through indexing a set of markdown files and running your first semantic query.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: pip install indexio

docs.title: 1. Initialize a config
docs.text:
indexio init

This writes a starter config to .indexio/config.yaml with a docs/**/*.md source and a local ChromaDB store.

docs.title: 2. Build the index
docs.text:
indexio build

indexio reads every file matched by your source globs, splits them into chunks, generates embeddings, and stores them in ChromaDB.

docs.title: 3. Query
docs.text:
indexio query "how does authentication work"

Results show the most semantically similar chunks, with corpus, source path, and a snippet.

docs.title: 4. Check status
docs.text:
indexio status

Shows the current store plus source state buckets like indexed, changed, and not yet built based on the last build manifest.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Register sources from other packages
  • Run the chat server
  • Configuration reference
